title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Select thread, memory, leak, race, or platform sanitizers according to risk and compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Asan · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Asan without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use addresssanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.

content.text: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Asan, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Passing ordinary tests proves memory access is safe. This fails the Asan contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: TypeScript TSConfig Reference (TypeScript 5.9) — Controls strict typing, unused code checks, indexed access, and compiler gate behavior.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Sanitizer Ci applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run expensive dynamic checks in a documented matrix with symbolized actionable output.
